name: WebSocket Order Update (Subscribe/Updates/Unsubscribe)
summary: WebSocket order-update feed over the Flattrade PiConnect socket. The client subscribes by sending a frame with t="o" plus the account id; the server then pushes order-update messages (t="om") for every order event (Fill, Rejected, Canceled, etc.) on that account. There is NO acknowledgement frame for the subscribe request itself. Unsubscribe by sending a frame with t="uo", which the server acknowledges with t="uok".

json_fields.name: t
json_fields.description: Task type. Send 'o' to subscribe to order updates ('o' represents order update subscription task); send 'uo' to unsubscribe ('uo' represents Unsubscribe Order update).

json_fields.name: actid
json_fields.description: Account id based on which order updated to be sent. Sent in the subscribe (t="o") frame.

response_fields.name: t
response_fields.description: Message type. 'om' represents the order-update feed push message (the doc text labels it 'touchline feed' verbatim, which is a copy-paste artifact); 'uok' represents the Unsubscribe Order update acknowledgement. NOTE: there is no acknowledgement for the order-update subscription (t="o") request itself.

response_fields.name: status
response_fields.description: Order status (New, Replaced, Complete, Rejected etc).

response_fields.name: reporttype
response_fields.description: Order event for which this message is sent out. (Fill, Rejected, Canceled)

response_fields.name: trantype
response_fields.description: Order transaction type, buy or sell.

response_fields.name: fillshares
response_fields.description: Total Filled shares for this order.

response_fields.name: fltm
response_fields.description: Fill Time (present only when reporttype is Fill).

response_fields.name: flid
response_fields.description: Fill ID (present only when reporttype is Fill).

response_fields.name: flqty
response_fields.description: Fill Qty (present only when reporttype is Fill).

response_fields.name: flprc
response_fields.description: Fill Price (present only when reporttype is Fill).

response_fields.name: cancelqty
response_fields.description: Canceled quantity, in case of canceled order.

response_fields.name: remarks
response_fields.description: User added tag, while placing order.

response_fields.name: snonum
response_fields.description: This will be present for child orders in case of cover and bracket orders, if present needs to be sent during exit.

response_fields.name: snoordt
response_fields.description: This will be present for child orders in case of cover and bracket orders, it will indicate whether the order is profit or stoploss.

response_fields.name: blprc
response_fields.description: This will be present for cover and bracket parent order. This is the differential stop loss trigger price to be entered.

response_fields.name: bpprc
response_fields.description: This will be present for bracket parent order. This is the differential profit price to be entered.

response_fields.name: trailprc
response_fields.description: This will be present for cover and bracket parent order. This is required if trailing ticks is to be enabled.

response_fields.name: exch_tm
response_fields.description: This will have the exchange update time. Format: dd-mm-YYYY hh:MM:ss.

response_fields.name: amo
response_fields.description: This field will be present if the order is After Market Order. Data will be "Yes".

response_fields.name: rejby
response_fields.description: If an order is rejected, it will indicate from where it got rejected.

response_fields.name: dname
response_fields.description: Broker specific contract display name, present only if applicable.

notes: This is a WebSocket (wss://) endpoint over the PiConnect socket, not REST; the doc illustrates the frames as JSON sent over the socket via a `curl --data` example. The order-update section spans PDF pages 77-79 (page 80 begins a separate 'Subscribe Position Update' endpoint). IMPORTANT: there is NO subscription acknowledgement frame for the order-update subscription (t="o") - the server simply starts pushing t="om" messages. By contrast, the unsubscribe (t="uo") IS acknowledged with t="uok". The subscribe frame carries actid (account id); the unsubscribe frame carries only t. The om-feed `t` row description reads "'om' represents touchline feed" verbatim in the source - a doc copy-paste artifact (it is the order-update feed). No field in either the page images or the cleaned text carries a trailing '*', so NO field is doc-marked mandatory; the required flag on `t` is inferred (the frame is meaningless without it) and actid is required only for the subscribe frame. Several order-update (om) fields are conditional: fltm/flid/flqty/flprc are present only when reporttype is Fill; cancelqty appears only for canceled orders; trgprc only for SL orders; snonum/snoordt only for cover/bracket child orders; blprc/trailprc for cover & bracket parent orders; bpprc for bracket parent order; amo present only for After Market Orders (value "Yes"); dname present only if applicable. exch_tm time format is dd-mm-YYYY hh:MM:ss. The reporttype "Canceled" / status spellings ("Canceled", "Replaced", "Complete") are reproduced verbatim from the doc. No sample success/failure JSON response bodies are provided in the doc for this WebSocket section (only the curl request frames are shown), so those fields are left empty.
issues: The cleaned text labels the t="om" feed description as "'om' represents touchline feed" - reproduced verbatim; this is a doc copy-paste artifact (it is the order-update feed, not a touchline feed). No field in this section carries a trailing '*' in either the image or text, so no field is doc-marked mandatory; required flags are inferred from the subscribe/unsubscribe frame semantics. No sample success/failure JSON response bodies are provided in the doc for this WebSocket section (only the curl request frames are shown). The om-message possible-value cells are empty in the source for most fields; the enum-like values shown were derived from inline parenthetical text in the Description column where present (status, reporttype, trantype, prctyp, ret, handlinst, amo, exch_tm). Page-range note: section content genuinely ends on page 79; page 80 starts the separate 'Subscribe Position Update' endpoint.
